title: Fix login issue — user cannot log in to the app

requirements.summary: Audit and fix the Internet Identity login flow in ChatView and useInternetIdentity so that a user can successfully initiate and complete login
acceptanceCriteria: A clearly visible login button is rendered when the user is not authenticated.
Clicking the login button opens the Internet Identity authentication popup without throwing any console errors.
After successful authentication, the app transitions out of the unauthenticated state and renders either the DisplayNamePrompt/ProfileSetupModal (new users) or the main ChatView UI (returning users).
If the login popup is cancelled or fails, the app returns to the login screen gracefully without crashing.
No unhandled promise rejections or console errors occur during the full login lifecycle.

file_operations.description: Audit and fix the authentication state rendering logic to ensure a visible login button is always shown when the user is unauthenticated. Remove any conditional logic that might hide the login UI. Ensure proper error boundaries are in place to catch and display authentication errors gracefully. Verify that after successful login the component correctly transitions to either profile setup (for new users) or the main chat UI (for returning users) based on the userProfile state.

Audit the login method implementation to ensure it properly handles the AuthClient.login call, catches and propagates errors appropriately, and updates the identity state correctly after successful authentication. Add explicit error handling for common failure cases (popup cancelled, network errors, timeout). Ensure the clear/logout method properly resets all identity state. Verify the component usage instructions from the authorization component are followed correctly.

Review and strengthen the actor availability guard to ensure the submit button remains disabled and shows a clear message if the actor is not yet initialized after login. Add retry logic or a manual retry button if actor initialization fails. Ensure error messages are user-friendly and actionable.

requirements.summary: Audit the useActor hook initialisation sequence to ensure the backend actor is created with the correct authenticated identity after login and add retry mechanism for failures
acceptanceCriteria: After a successful Internet Identity login, the useActor hook produces a non-null actor reference within a reasonable time (e.g., 5 seconds).
If actor initialisation fails, an inline error message is displayed and the user is not left on a blank or spinning screen.
Authenticated actor calls (e.g., getUserProfile) succeed after login without 'actor is not available' errors.

file_operations.description: Audit the actor creation logic to ensure it correctly receives and uses the authenticated identity from useInternetIdentity after login. Add a timeout mechanism (e.g., 5-10 seconds) to detect if actor initialization is taking too long. Implement a retry mechanism with exponential backoff if the initial actor creation fails. Expose an error state that components can use to display user-facing error messages. Ensure the React Query setup properly handles the actor dependency and refetches when the identity changes.

Integrate the actor error state from useActor and display a clear inline error message with a retry button if actor initialization fails after login. Prevent rendering the main chat UI or profile setup flow until the actor is confirmed available. Show a loading state with a timeout indicator if actor initialization is taking longer than expected.

Review all query hooks to ensure they properly handle the case where the actor is temporarily unavailable after login. Add enabled flags that check both actor availability and authentication state. Ensure queries do not execute and fail silently before the actor is ready. Add proper error handling that surfaces backend errors to the UI layer.
